content:
Can you carify what you mean by "nothing is going into any of the sent folders that I have sent"?

A. You're not receiving any emails for a particular account. B. You're not receiving emails which you expect to arrive/show in a folder other than "Inbox". C. After sending an email, it is not being moved to the "sent mail" folder.

For point B, you need to enable sync on any custom folders you have. To do this...

1. Open the account list. 2. Select the applicable account. 3. Press "manage folders" (bottom of list). 4. Tap desired folder. 5. Toggle "sync" on.

Repeat for each required folder.

To manually sync accounts ... 1. Open the account list. 2. Tap the down arrow. 3. Tap the "Sync all accounts" button.

Please also ensure the app is excluded from any android battery optimisation and allowed to run in the background.

1. Long press the Thunderbird launcher icon. 2. Select "App Info" 3. Tap battery usage. 4. Toggle "Allow background activity" to ON
creator: platform34

content:
Thunderbird Android is built upon K-9 Mail, rather that a new app written from scratch. It's code base is about 15 years old and the team are working to modernise this (significant work involved). K-9 mail featured only a unified Inbox.

It's on Thunderbird Android's roadmap to extend the feature with unified Outbox, Drafts, Sent, Spam, and Trash folders, as outlined in this link: https://github.com/thunderbird/thunderbird-android/issues/10203

You can show support for the feature by voting for it on Mozilla Connect, linked below. https://connect.mozilla.org/t5/ideas/thunderbird-mobile-unified-folder-for-inbox-drafts-sent-spam/idi-p/95546

content:
Two things I'd recommend checking.

a) Have you excluded the app from any android battery optimisations and allowed it to run in the background. This is a common reason for email's not synchronising in a timely manner.

  1. Long press the app launcher icon > app info > battery usage.
  2. Toggle "Allow background activity" to ON.

b) Each folder you wish to sync automatically must have it's sync setting turned on (Inbox on by default).

  1. Open the account list.
  2. Tap the applicable account.
  3. Tap manage folders (bottom of list).
  4. Tap folder's name.
  5. Toggle "Sync" to ON.

Hi Steve Do you use the Unified inbox. Check "Unify" is not enabled on the "All Mail" folder. It should be disabled. This is the most common reason to observe duplicates.

  1. Open the account list.
  2. Tap account name
  3. Tap manage folders (bottom of list)

Some providers such as gmail allow labels to be assigned to an email. If an email is assigned more than one label, it can appear duplicated as Thunderbird treats labels as seperate folders.

content:
Yahoo no longer supports basic authentication (account user name and password) for email client sign-in.

You need to use either oAuth 2.0 (modern prefered method) or setup a 2FA App password.

oAuth 2.0

In Thunderbird, navigate to ... 1. Settings > account > fetching mail > incoming server 2. Settings > account > sending mail > outgoung server

a. Change authentication type = oAuth 2.0, then next. b. Click the yahoo "sign-in" button. c. A yahoo sign-in web page will open. d. login and grant Thunderbird the required permissions


App password

AI generated steps.

Web Browser Steps Log into your Yahoo Mail account at mail.yahoo.com. Click your profile icon (top-right), select "Account info," then go to "Account security" on the left. Under "Other ways to sign in," click "Generate and manage app passwords," select "Get started," enter a name like "IMAP Client" or "Outlook," and click "Generate." Copy the 16-character password (shown once) and use it in your email client's password field instead of your regular password

content:
Yahoo no longer supports using simple authentication (your main account user name and password). You will need to create an app password for Thunderbird mobile via your Yahoo webmail settings. See steps below.

The pop3 protocol doesn't have a concept of sync. It downloads a local copy of unread email to the email client, then typically deletes the copy on the server.

If you need to retain a copy of emails on the server so Thunderbird Desktop can download them, you need to change the settings on Yahoo's webmail pop3 settings page and within Thunderbird mobile to define how deletions should be managed.

Imap on the other hand keeps emails in sync with the servers. It recommended on mobile and can be used in parrallel with pop3 on the desktop.

AI generated guide.

Create a Yahoo app password (web browser) 1. Open a browser and go to Yahoo Mail and sign in. 2. Click your profile icon (top right) and choose Account info. 3. Go to the Account Security (or Security) section. 4. Scroll to Other ways to sign in or External connections. 5. Click App passwords or Generate and manage app passwords (wording can vary). 6. Click Generate app password / Create app password. 7. Type a name for the app (for example, “Outlook on PC” or “iPhone Mail”) and confirm/generate. 8. Yahoo will show you a 16‑character password in a popup. 9. Copy this password and use it in the email app instead of your normal Yahoo password.

content:
Are you highlighting an issue you have or a feature you'd like to see?

The Android client is not designed to sync with your desktop client, it downloads emails from your mail providers server.

Is your android client connecting to the server using Imap (prefered) or the pop3 protocol?

Imap keeps your emails in sync with the server. To enable sync for one or more folder ...

  1. Open the account list
  2. Tap on the applicable account.
  3. Tap manage folders (bottom of list).
  4. Tap desired folder name.
  5. Toggle "sync" to ON.
  6. Repeat for each desired folder.

Pop3 was intended for use with only a single email client. It simply downloads a local copy of new emails and has no concept of folder sync. After an email downloads, it's typically deleted on the server. You can change how these deletions are managed through a combination of app and your providers webmail settings.

To find these settings in Thunderbird for Android; Navigate to settings > account > fetching mail ...

  • When I delete a message (pick from options)
  • Erase deleted messages on server (pick from options)

content:
Thunderbird on Android doesn't have a feature to highlight or provide push notifications on an individual email basis.

For the push notification, can I suggest a workaround that achieves your objective. Setup a server-side rule (via your webmail) that moves new emails from the sender to a dedicated folder.

You can skip the above step if you're happy to receive push notifications from new emails hitting Inbox, or folder(s) of your choice.

In the Thunderbird Android app, enable push notifications on your desired folder(s).

  1. Open the account list
  2. Tap account name
  3. Tap manage folders (bottom of list)
  4. Tap 3 dot menu
  5. Tap refresh folder list
  6. Tap folder name
  7. Toggle "Enable Push" ON

On Android 13 and later, grant Thunderbird the "alarm and reminders" permission, required for the push service.

See the guide linked below. https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/configure-push-email-thunderbird-android

Android restricts apps with background sync. The interval can't be less that 15 minutes. The push service is recommended thereafter, and is the reason why push has a constatant forground notification.

You can find "folder poll frequency" under settings > account > fetching mail

You can raise any feature ideas you have on Mozilla connect, where they are opened to votes from the community.

https://connect.mozilla.org/t5/ideas/idb-p/ideas/label-name/thunderbird%20android/tab/most-kudoed
creator: platform34

Internet reports seem to point to server side issues, rather than a client issue.

  • A Microsoft-side IMAP server issue or misconfiguration.
  • Temporary server rejection due to high load or brief service interruption.
  • Throttling when a client sends many IMAP commands in a short time.
  • Older exchange server that doesn't support newer IMAP commands such as ID and Move.

What errors are you seeing in Thunderbird for Android that leads you to believe it's being blocked. Are you a business hosting your own mail server?

CBL blacklists help mail companies decide whether to accept or reject "Incoming" mail to their server. It doesn't typically block users from recieving email in their mail client.

Are you using any kind of mobile antivirus such as Avast mobile security. Another user with a similar issue found disabling it's web guard feature resolved the issue.

Have you tried rebooting your router? ISPs often issue home users their public IP by DHCP. The reboot should assign you a different IP address.

content:
Is this a new account you've added to TB-A or errors that just started occuring on a previously working configuration?

The lack of a password field would be normal for oAuth 2.0 authentication. Plain user name and password authentication is no longer supported by Yahoo.

Try logging into your webmail, send yourself an email to confirm the account is functioning normally. It might be a temporary service issue or a full mailbox.

creator: platform34

content:
In android ...

  1. Open settings > apps > gmail.
  2. Tap "open by default" label.
  3. Tap the "clear default preferences" button.

The exact wording and menu location can vary by phone manufacturer & Android version. After the default preference is cleared, you will be prompted which app to open mailto links with.
